ai.title: Promotion Day celebrated at Vivekanand Millennium School
ai.description: ‘Samvardhanam 2026’ — The Promotion Day at St Vivekanand Millennium School, Pinjore, was a vibrant and heart-warming celebration that beautifully captured the spirit of students’ growth, efforts and achievements. Held at Dr BR Ambedkar Auditorium, the event transformed the traditional result day into a joyful and unforgettable experience where every child felt valued, motivated and truly celebrated. The programme began with a soulful rendition of Vande Mataram, followed by an energetic celebration that lit up the stage with rhythmic music and dazzling cold pyros, setting an enthusiastic tone for the day. As excitement built up, students eagerly waited to hear their names. The moment their names were announced with lively music, they walked onto the stage with great confidence. Their success was celebrated in a grand manner with sparkling effects and cheerful applause, making each child feel special and proud. The major highlights of the event were the beautifully arranged 360-degree selfie stand and other selfie corners where students and parents captured joyful moments together in their graceful convocation attire. These vibrant setups became the centre of attraction, preserving unforgettable memories of this milestone day. Bringing smiles all around, cartoon characters interacted with students, adding a playful and joyous vibe to the celebration. Adding a futuristic touch, the school recognised students’ participation in skill-based learning. Certifications in advanced domains such as Blockchain, Artificial Intelligence, Coding & Programming, and Cyber Security — awarded in collaboration with IBM and TransNeuron —reflected the school’s commitment to preparing students for the future. The Parent Appreciation Card Initiative added an emotional touch, as parents shared heartfelt messages of pride and encouragement, strengthening bonds and boosting students’ confidence. Parents also appreciated the school’s dedicated efforts in nurturing their children’s overall development. Students also expressed their gratitude towards their parents, teachers and school, reflecting happiness, confidence and a strong determination to move ahead. Principal Dr Piyush Punj emphasised that education goes beyond marks, focusing on building confident and responsible individuals.

ai.title: Ecommerce, quick commerce chasing ad spends with profitability in focus
ai.description: Online platforms like Amazon, Flipkart, Blinkit, Instamart, Zepto, Zomato, and Swiggy are set to earn substantial advertising revenue. This growth is fueled by integrated shopping experiences and advanced AI insights. Quick commerce is experiencing rapid expansion, with brands shifting ad spends for faster results.

ai.title: Oracle’s AI pivot cuts deep, lays off 20% of its India workforce
ai.description: Oracle has cut around 10,000 jobs in India, sources told ET. This is part of a global restructuring affecting 30,000 employees. The company is reducing roles in its tech divisions. Oracle Financial Software Services also saw job losses. Employees are concerned about increased workloads. This follows similar tech industry layoffs this year.

(Headquarters: Minato-ku, Tokyo; President, CEO: Ryo Higuchi; Stock Code: 3491; hereinafter referred to as the 'Company') announces that its AI real estate investment service, 'RENOSY,' has launched an initiative to rebrand 'Renters Warehouse' (operated by RW OpCo, LLC [Headquarters: United States], a Group company), as 'RENOSY by Renters Warehouse.'

ai.title: Is it time to buy IT ? Fund managers suggest gradual addition by investors
ai.description: Indian IT stocks are at their cheapest since mid-2020, presenting a buying opportunity. Fund managers suggest gradual accumulation over two years as valuations have significantly compressed. Despite concerns over AI and recent sell-offs, the sector's improving deal momentum and expanding market offer potential for value investors. A balanced approach combining lump sum and staggered investments is advised.
